title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Olga Seminova, an exchange student, argues with Mr. Fullerton regarding a disputed will left by the deceased Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. Olga claims that Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith wrote the document herself, noting that the mistress had previously made Olga practice imitating her handwriting to maintain Victorian social standards for private letters. Olga asserts that the cleaning lady and a gardener named Jim witnessed the signing.

However, Mr. Fullerton reveals that an expert handwriting analysis concluded the codicil was not written by Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. This suspicion was initially raised by his associate, Small Cole, who noted the handwriting did not match Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith's older files. Mr. Fullerton believes Olga’s greed led her to create a will that excluded all relatives, including a long-term beneficiary nephew, in favor of Olga herself. While moved by Olga's tragic past of loss and injustice, Fullerton maintains that the evidence is against her. He advises her to "come clean" to avoid harsher consequences, suggesting she might receive probation due to being a first-time offender and a non-fluent foreigner.

In response, Olga expresses feelings of persecution and contemplates fleeing Britain using fake papers or disguises, claiming she has saved enough money to disappear. Mr. Fullerton dismisses her plan as childish, warning that an arrest warrant would find her. Following this exchange, Hercule Poirot arrives at an estate, where he is told Mrs. Dreyfus will soon arrive. While waiting in the living room, Poirot observes a meticulously maintained garden filled with asters, chrysanthemums, and roses.
